questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: $\begin{aligned} & a x+b y+c=0 \\ & b x+c y+a=0 \\ & c x+a y+b=0\end{aligned}$ is a system of linear equation then answer the following questions :Question: If $\mathrm{a}=\mathrm{b}=\mathrm{c}$ and $a+b+c \neq 0$ then the system of linear equation have -
options: infinite solution all lying on a line
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{a}=\mathrm{b}=\mathrm{c}$ makes all the lines coincident
$a(x+y+1)=0$
$a \neq 0$ because $a+b+c \neq 0$
$x+y+1=0 \Rightarrow$ infinite solutions, all lying on the line $x+y+1=0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the system of linear equation $\begin{aligned} & x-2 y+b z=3 \\ & a x+2 z=2 \\ & 5 x+2 y=1\end{aligned}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question: If $a b \neq 12$ then system of linear equations has -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x-2 y+b z=3 \\ & a x+2 z=2 \\ & 5 x+2 y=1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -2 & b \\ a & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right|=-24+2 a b \\ & \Delta_1=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -2 & 3 \\ a & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & 2 & 1\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_1=8 a-24 \\ & \Delta_2=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & b \\ a & 2 & 2 \\ 5 & 1 & 0\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_2=28+a b-10 b . \\ & \Delta_3=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & b \\ 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_3=16+4 b .\end{aligned}$
$a b \neq 12 \Rightarrow \Delta \neq 0$
$\Rightarrow$ system has unique solution.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the system of linear equation $\begin{aligned} & x-2 y+b z=3 \\ & a x+2 z=2 \\ & 5 x+2 y=1\end{aligned}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question: If $a=3 \& b=4$ then system of equations has -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x-2 y+b z=3 \\ & a x+2 z=2 \\ & 5 x+2 y=1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -2 & b \\ a & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right|=-24+2 a b \\ & \Delta_1=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -2 & 3 \\ a & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & 2 & 1\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_1=8 a-24 \\ & \Delta_2=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & b \\ a & 2 & 2 \\ 5 & 1 & 0\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_2=28+a b-10 b . \\ & \Delta_3=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & b \\ 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_3=16+4 b .\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & a=3 \& b=4 \\ & \Rightarrow \Delta=\Delta_1=\Delta_2=\Delta_3=0\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow$ system has infinite solution.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the system of linear equation $\begin{aligned} & x-2 y+b z=3 \\ & a x+2 z=2 \\ & 5 x+2 y=1\end{aligned}$ On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question: If $\mathrm{ab}=12$ and $\mathrm{a} \neq 3$ then system of linear equations has -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x-2 y+b z=3 \\ & a x+2 z=2 \\ & 5 x+2 y=1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -2 & b \\ a & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right|=-24+2 a b \\ & \Delta_1=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -2 & 3 \\ a & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & 2 & 1\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_1=8 a-24 \\ & \Delta_2=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 3 & b \\ a & 2 & 2 \\ 5 & 1 & 0\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_2=28+a b-10 b . \\ & \Delta_3=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}3 & -2 & b \\ 2 & 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta_3=16+4 b .\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & a b=12 \Rightarrow \Delta=0 \\ & a \neq 3 \Rightarrow \Delta_1 \neq 0\end{aligned}$
Hence no solution
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: $\Delta=\left|\begin{array}{lll}a_1 & a_2 & a_3 \\ b_1 & b_2 & b_3 \\ d_1 & d_2 & d_3\end{array}\right|$ $M_{i j}=$ Minor of the element of $\mathrm{i}^{\text {th }}$ row and $j^{\text {th }}$ column $C_{i j}=$ Cofactor of the element of $\mathrm{i}^{\text {th }}$ row and $\mathrm{j}^{\text {th }}$ columnQuestion: Value of $b_1 \cdot C_{31}+b_2 \cdot C_{32}+b_3 \cdot C_{33}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & b_1 \cdot c_{31}+b_2 \cdot c_{32}+b_3 \cdot c_{33} \\ & b_1\left|\begin{array}{ll}a_2 & a_3 \\ b_2 & b_3\end{array}\right|-b_2\left|\begin{array}{ll}a_1 & a_3 \\ b_1 & b_3\end{array}\right|+b_3\left|\begin{array}{ll}a_1 & a_2 \\ b_1 & b_2\end{array}\right|=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: $\Delta=\left|\begin{array}{lll}a_1 & a_2 & a_3 \\ b_1 & b_2 & b_3 \\ d_1 & d_2 & d_3\end{array}\right|$ $M_{i j}=$ Minor of the element of $\mathrm{i}^{\text {th }}$ row and $j^{\text {th }}$ column $C_{i j}=$ Cofactor of the element of $\mathrm{i}^{\text {th }}$ row and $\mathrm{j}^{\text {th }}$ columnQuestion: $a_3 M_{13}-b_3 \cdot M_{23}+d_3 \cdot M_{33}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $a_3 M_{13}-b_3 \cdot M_{23}+d_3 \cdot M_{33}=a_3 C_{13}+b_3 \cdot C_{23}+d_3 \cdot C_{33}=\Delta$ by definition
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x), g(x)$ and $h(x)$ be function of degree more than 5 and $\Delta(x)$ $\Delta(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}x f(x)+f^{\prime}(\alpha) & x g(x)+g^{\prime}(\alpha) & x h(x)+h^{\prime}(\alpha) \\ -2 f(\alpha)+3 f^{\prime}(\alpha) & -2 g(\alpha)+3 g^{\prime}(\alpha) & -2 h(\alpha)+3 h^{\prime}(\alpha) \\ f^{\prime}(\alpha) & g^{\prime}(\alpha) & h^{\prime}(\alpha)\end{array}\right|$Question: If $f(x), g(x), h(x)$ are even function $\Delta(x)$ will not give remainder 0 when divided by
questions.solution:
By applying $R_1 \rightarrow R_1-R_3, R_2 \rightarrow R_2-3 R_3$
$\Delta(x)=-2 x\left|\begin{array}{lll}f(x) & g(x) & h(x) \\ f(\alpha) & g(\alpha) & h(\alpha) \\ f^{\prime}(\alpha) & g^{\prime}(\alpha) & h^{\prime}(\alpha)\end{array}\right|$
Clearly $\Delta(\alpha)=0, \Delta^{\prime}(\alpha)=0$
As $\mathrm{f}, \mathrm{g}$, h are function $\Delta(-\alpha)=0, \Delta^{\prime}(-\alpha)=0$
Means $\Delta(x)$ has factors $x,(x-\alpha)^2,(x-\alpha)^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x), g(x)$ and $h(x)$ be function of degree more than 5 and $\Delta(x)$ $\Delta(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}x f(x)+f^{\prime}(\alpha) & x g(x)+g^{\prime}(\alpha) & x h(x)+h^{\prime}(\alpha) \\ -2 f(\alpha)+3 f^{\prime}(\alpha) & -2 g(\alpha)+3 g^{\prime}(\alpha) & -2 h(\alpha)+3 h^{\prime}(\alpha) \\ f^{\prime}(\alpha) & g^{\prime}(\alpha) & h^{\prime}(\alpha)\end{array}\right|$Question: Let $\alpha=1$ and $f(x)=x^5+2 x^4, g(x)=x^7-x^8, h(x)=1+x-x^9$ then remainder when. $\Delta(x)$ is divided by $x^2-1$
questions.solution:
By applying $R_1 \rightarrow R_1-R_3, R_2 \rightarrow R_2-3 R_3$
$\Delta(x)=-2 x\left|\begin{array}{lll}f(x) & g(x) & h(x) \\ f(\alpha) & g(\alpha) & h(\alpha) \\ f^{\prime}(\alpha) & g^{\prime}(\alpha) & h^{\prime}(\alpha)\end{array}\right|$
Clearly $\Delta(\alpha)=0, \Delta^{\prime}(\alpha)=0$
As $\Delta(\mathrm{x})$ is divided by $\mathrm{x}^2-1$ remainder will be ax +b (linear)
$\begin{aligned} & \Delta(x)=\left(x^2-1\right) Q(x)+a x+b \\ & \Delta(1)=a+b=0 \\ & \Delta(-1)=-a+b=-352 \\ & a x+b=176(x-1)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
